claim: Assembly of invadopodia is triggered by focal generation of phosphatidylinositol-3,4-biphosphate and the activation of the nonreceptor tyrosine kinase Src.

result.claim: Assembly of invadopodia is triggered by focal generation of phosphatidylinositol-3,4-biphosphate and the activation of the nonreceptor tyrosine kinase Src.
result.reasoning: Paper 16280642 directly supports the claim by demonstrating that Src expression stimulates podosome formation (which are functionally similar to invadopodia in cancer cells) after PtdIns(3,4)P2 accumulation. The paper explicitly states that podosomes in Src-transformed cells are enriched with PtdIns(3,4)P2, highlighting its important role, and that Src-expression triggers their formation following PtdIns(3,4)P2 accumulation.

evidence.text: The invadopodia of metastatic carcinoma cells have four distinct stages of assembly and maturation: invadopodium precursor formation, actin polymerization, stabilization, and matrix degradation.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: Invadopodia that formed upon MT1-MMP inhibition or depletion retained actin-cortactin accumulations but were unable to degrade matrix.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Organization of invadopodia structure requires cortactin.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Cofilin severs actin filaments to create barbed ends at invadopodia to support Arp2/3-dependent actin polymerization.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: Inositol 1,4,5-triphosphate receptor 1 and myosin light chain kinase were recruited as key phosphorylation targets of presynaptic PKG-I in nociceptive neurons.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Cortactin phosphorylation regulates cofilin and Arp2/3 complex-dependent actin polymerization in invadopodia.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: In phosphoenolpyruvate carboxykinase (PEPCK)-GFP mice, serial MPM found PEC-to-podocyte migration.
evidence.source: Tracking the fate of glomerular epithelial cells in vivo using serial multiphoton imaging in novel mouse models with fluorescent lineage tags

evidence.text: Podosomes are structures functionally similar to invadopodia.
evidence.source: Membrane lipids in invadopodia and podosomes: Key structures for cancer invasion and metastasis

evidence.text: There are four distinct invadopodial stages: membrane cortactin aggregation at membranes adherent to matrix, MT1-MMP accumulation at the region of cortactin accumulation, matrix degradation at the invadopodia region, and subsequent cortactin dissociation from the area of continued MT1-MMP accumulation associated with foci of degraded matrix.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Either protease inhibition or MT1-MMP siRNA depletion moderately decreased the formation of invadopodial structures.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Invadopodia are matrix-degrading membrane protrusions in invasive carcinoma cells.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: Invadopodial structures were identified as actin-cortactin accumulations at the ventral cell membrane adherent to matrix.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Invadopodia degrade extracellular matrix (ECM) barriers.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Invadopodia are extracellular matrix (ECM)-degrading protrusions formed by invasive cancer cells.
evidence.source: Membrane lipids in invadopodia and podosomes: Key structures for cancer invasion and metastasis

evidence.text: Somatic mutations activate phosphoinositide 3-kinase (PI3K) in the p110-alpha catalytic subunit (encoded by PIK3CA).
evidence.source: Effective Use of PI3K and MEK Inhibitors to Treat Mutant K-Ras G12D and PIK3CA H1047R Murine Lung Cancers

evidence.text: Inhibition of invadopodial structure assembly by cortactin depletion resulted in a block of matrix degradation.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Invadopodia are membrane protrusions.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: Tks5/FISH is essential for podosome formation.
evidence.source: Sequential signals toward podosome formation in NIH-src cells

evidence.text: Loss of PTEN and INPP4B leads to synergistic accumulation of PI(3,4)P2.
evidence.source: PTEN Regulates PI(3,4)P2 Signaling Downstream of Class I PI3K

evidence.text: Coexpression of Tks5/Fish and Src in epithelial cells resulted in the appearance of podosomes.
evidence.source: The adaptor protein Tks5/Fish is required for podosome formation and function, and for the protease-driven invasion of cancer cells.

evidence.text: Src-expression stimulated podosome formation at focal adhesions of NIH3T3 cells after PtdIns(3,4)P2 accumulation.
evidence.source: Sequential signals toward podosome formation in NIH-src cells
